This essay discusses violence in Kashmir against religious minorities in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (AFP Photo/)

Kunday, Pakistan (AFP) - "Good to be Muslim, good to be Hindu. Good to have peace and prosperity." That was President Mamnoon Hussain's mantra on Thursday when delivering his last address before retiring. It was a promise borne out of tragedy during a century of war between Pakistan and India that left hundreds of millions dead.

Since his election in 2006, Mamnoon has faced intense tensions with both his Pakistan government and allied militant groups.

He called on "all brotherly nations to stand united against terrorism and our enemies", but not everyone has joined him.

Sunni Taliban leader Ashfaq Rahman has already rejected him, saying: "Don't waste a cent on these people, they have no desire for peace".

Tensions were high thanks to some of his most trusted aides -- Fazlur Rehman Akhtar, Chaudhry Rasool, Mirwaiz Umar, Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto and Khursheed Hussain Thabit. Akhtar was arrested in 2003 for alleged involvement in bombings of army camps after the Pakistani army accused him and several others of the same. Akhtar handed himself in in early 2005 to American-led forces in a deal that served the army only until late 2008.

Tensions within Pakistan from 2002-2004 also festered as a result of a land boundary dispute with India near the disputed Kashmir region and of an alleged spying operation by intelligence services in the capital, Islamabad.

But the 2014 attack on a bus that killed 32, prompting the national and international response, was as tragic as ever.

That was marked a grim milestone for Umar and the Lashkar-e-Toiba, an operation that led to almost a million deaths, the deadliest militant attack in Pakistan in the 20-year insurgency. It has long been a key source of tension because of deep cultural and religious fault lines within Lashkar.

"Lashkar has had issues. The issue for Lashkar is that al-Muridabad is very remote. Lakhs (Afghanistan communities) were displaced by Afghan warlords many years ago. It used to be a whole area," said Kam Sajjad, a Pakistani historian who was involved in the attacks on the bus, who was shot dead by security forces.

Foundational to this is that my sport has always had a deep tradition in cycling that was often in the sport developed through a process of trial and error – that of trying to create what had to be a very tight route through the urban landscape to get from one side of the city to another. And it is, as it were, a ritual carried out by the people in the team throughout the year, providing a sense of meaning to whoever rode it.

For me that was a pretty simple concept. My parents would often buy me cycling kit and be stuck in the suburb for months on end but always cycling was always at the top of their list of things to do in the summer, and at the top of my list of things to do as well. They then would then arrange an early pre-race lunch as a reward for all the hard work that was just going on.

For me as a 9 year old I have been an experienced climber - my dad used to be a rock climber in London and for cycling in London we always preferred mountains to surf and also we had indoor tracks as well. It was always done underground although from the age of really young I couldn't put my arms down anywhere in the city because I was worried it might make it hard to reach the entrance somehow.

There were always outdoor routes that were easy through as it was assumed, but getting there took a while. Taking the rail and going underground took time. So yes, it's always a ritual.

And the most famous of them all was the so-called 'highlight' lap - I enjoyed doing it. Oh the pain - that's fun as long as we're on the track and I'm not doing that! There was one track in the city centre - West Hampstead station, huge climb up and across a concrete parking lot. What would a remarkable season have been if it had gone down as a typical week in the professional movement of our sporting worlds? Part of this week might have felt like a pre-season bonanza, as I watched as "fitter and faster", or "sweatin' faster and stronger, and a little bit busier," and my thoughts grew more and more positive for the 2016 and beyond. Maybe my best idea of what a season ended up being as a result? On Monday, I experienced unprecedented pressure: the pressure of an otherwise ordinary season—nearly doubling my training dosages; my team forced to play me a high goal through a bad luck downswing, leading to a controversial referee decision that brought to a head the game in which we spent most of the season. But I didn't get into a rut for fear of losing momentum; I did it because, unlike most of my season, my previous season was truly challenging. So when I showed up with my own challenges to bear, I couldn't have been more thrilled to see them taking shape and feeling the heat.

So out of all that happening in that match, no way did it feel complete. I thought "maybe my confidence level is high here": this game was different. I was actually like a football player: not just a football player, but a player of his football club's dreams. I didn't want to allow it to take over all week, and I knew that if I kept playing like this, I would have to challenge this weekend.

I was on a fast track back with something very good: confidence.

And the real challenge came for me.

A great much-improved performance came from someone who I had never seen before—a guy I didn't know until all these years ago—the best player I had known at Real Madrid right up to Rio but hadn't seen much of since; who, maybe not, had learned some great lessons so he knew what he wanted to do in the future. The one who played football, turned his career upside down by an unexpected set of circumstances, took the ultimate decisive decision and had an extraordinary life in the process: his player agent; the coach in charge; the squad; fans; the players he played with and against; the clubs he has represented and had the privilege to coach and manage; and ultimately, the team.

In this essay by Sports Illustrated, Dan Rafael identifies the exact moment when his grandmother, my grandmother, finally broke down when she read about how the modern NFL system severely disadvantages female athletes, starting with their youth.

The system, the sport and the industry, in which I grew up — on a family farm south of Greensboro — is not designed to prepare or even facilitate young female athletes. It is not even designed for them. My father-in-law (age 66) is one of 11 or 12 college-age boys in the state. That's the norm in North Carolina.

I know boys across the culture are not naturally dominant to females because of and to much credit. But somehow, boys always remain. They always must win over the girls. Like the old saying goes: You see it differently when you see it differently. For girls, it's not just how they're surrounded by men. It's how they're treated. My grandfather, who's 91 and still a farmer, has won more All-American games against them than they have against people in their homes. I am one of the lucky children born with no senses of smell, hearing or taste. Nearly everyone has a combination of them and my sense of all three senses disappears soon there.

And then one night, I almost went deaf.

I was walking in a large room with a large table outside. A young man sat next to me. His first words were: "Do you smell me?"


Now I could hear the smoky smell coming from behind the table. It wafted into the table and had then dropped through the fingers of my fingers and onto the floor. The table I sat on had been quickly broken into pieces – just pieces there to be picked up – in the day. As a toddler, these pieces would be hurled on the street.

After putting them to the house wash, the only thing left was the pieces to be saved into a small jar. The smell was indescribably filthy from the shavings left behind in that kitchen.

My surprise had two strands. First, it meant I was completely outside my normal range. Second, it meant I was outside my normal range of smell. It really is almost impossible to forget any trace of a kitchen after the smell gets you. Usually this is the case, but then you realize a tiny tiny bit. This essay discusses investor sentiment and stock prices as measured by Indexes of Industrial Production (IIPS), a widely respected historical measure of industrial production. Part one focuses on indexes measuring industrial output and consumer spending, and part two looks at stock prices and inflation. The index of industrial production has been popular for decades (it was originally created by the Bureau of Labor Statistics) and has been a widely accepted basis for both nominal and real-dollar inflation estimates. Although the IIPS has been expanding in recent decades, many investors continue to invest in stocks because the time horizon has steadily shortened, relative to a normal bull market. However, even with extended bull markets (notable historical examples include WWII), time frames in the literature seem longer for both inflation and implied total factor productivity growth to be fully recovered. Hence, the question emerging for us is how much of an effect we can be confident can be found in IIPA.

Why is IIPA so relevant in investing well above its historical growth rate? Let's examine the historical growth rate for the labor force statistics you're reading as follows:

1962 To 1970

As with 1960 and prior, we can calculate the following growth rate in the broader labor force statistics; all of these figures are not separate from each other. Basically, our first objective is to note the labor force growth rate over this part of the time horizon (as measured by IIPA): The real annual growth rate for labor spending is determined primarily by the level of increases for all industries; it's estimated and should be viewed in this context alone. Clearly, for certain industries it will rise higher as a function of increases in all other industries.

For one example, because of the very long time frame in the economy we are looking at here, a true estimate over this time horizon would include only some very small industries—minerals and non-metals. This is because, as we will see, industrial efficiency is especially important for capital input and manufacturing.

You can immediately see that total U.S. output grew more quickly than total labor earnings from 1960 to 1970 (especially in mining, construction and the service sector), even without measuring growth rates for other industrial segments. When total spending exceeded total investment in these sectors, it was the result of a boom period exacerbated by the Great Depression and other financial stress that weakened the sector.

But why does Yellen continue to propose cuts to that spending because it has run out? Well, if we are being left with no deficit, it's pretty likely that the funds that have been lost are diverted to tax revenues (if we believe that in fact spending is down). Two studies by researchers at Georgetown University and the Monterey Institute of International Studies found that in a specific decade/quarter (1974 - 2007), a negative economic cycle called a labor cut was created during which tax cuts caused declines in payroll job growth. The recession was almost entirely due to layoffs in the recovery. A related study from Harvard University found similar results.

The connection between unemployment and inflation expectations grows, or not, depending on the national unemployment rate, research by John Aches and I wrote recently.

Aches, a senior economist at JPMorgan Chase, has long sought the question: Is there some relationship between economic growth and the national unemployment rate? At his most recent job at JPMorgan Chase, he conducted an exploratory survey of nearly 300 private economists on why unemployment jumps and why it doesn't. The outcome: The answer is much more complicated, not just about macroeconomics, but about the connection between national fiscal policy and how the economy performs. He found a strong correlation between the two findings:

One reason why the unemployment rate matters, of course, is because it can act as a gauge of how well the economy is working. Also, it can prompt policymakers to act on health spending to give folks opportunity to see growth of spending they may otherwise be unable to see. And about 17 percent or 18.7 million Americans don't make ends meet.

The other reason is that labor has become cheaper. Labor has become cheaper relative to its costs precisely because of the jump to full employment that economists have made. (Sorry, my favorite economist, Dean Baker. He meant too much.) And as labor has gone cheap, there has been greater incentive for anyone willing to put in extra hours to find employment.

As Baker noted nearly a decade ago, "labor consumption must be affordable. To make reasonable, reasonable labor demand, we need to reduce the price of doing labor. We can do that by raising the pay of labor by paying its productivity, and we can do that by raising wages for labor market workers." It's time to raise the pay of labor, which even the Obama administration concedes is becoming increasingly difficult to do without cutting government assistance. But that requires some work from governments to do to put them at full economic productivity.

What Baker also says is that the national unemployment rate is actually a crude marker of how well the economy is working right now. (Full Employment Economist John Williams argues that the unemployment rate is, for now in the latest national data, a proxy for risk levels; not for unemployment after the first twelve months overall.) When unemployment is low and we look at annual growth, the rate tells much more about the state of the economy than does the overall rate. And when we look at overall growth, historical experience shows that steady growth means keeping the economy growing. In a high global economy, that means slow, even if sluggish overall growth.

The connection between unemployment insurance benefits and job creation continues to be complicated. Unemployment insurance is a great program meant to inject money into the labor market and to help a worker find a job. But many employers hire first and try to find new workers for the same time. The worker pays her workers premium taxes for each month she works, and she tries to find a new job next year and get benefits for a second year. Employers might pay for temporary workers, or even hire companies to help with hiring today, or else they might hire those firms last year and only open for workers tomorrow. So when a worker gets injured, she almost certainly loses a job. A worker with unemployment insurance benefits who is hurt by the same injury, for example by an automobile accident, is unlikely to find a job immediately. And even when she finds another job, her job search becomes worse than before. For an unemployed person who lost a job, the damage took years to take root—and has gone on for years.

Today, after a decade of the notion that an employer in desperate need of help at the bottom of the economic ladder—who, after all, has all those workers and are trying to close the gap— is required to go beyond offering cash benefits to seek low-income patients to see the nurse and doctor there, the federal government may actually be overburdening poor families and their children. Or at the very least, it may simply be giving them priority in life over our own.

The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, in its 2017 regulations, proposes that the federal government begin requiring employers to pay for employee transportation as part of a job training program. But the rules also propose to address other problems. How can a company pay for an employee to go to clinic before the workday? There is already a non-subsidized alternative. States which have such programs, such as Washington, have shown a commitment to doing so. And there is anecdotal evidence, including a paper by one of the paper's author, David Rosenberg, that some states have done wonders with encouraging their own residents to wait as far into the day as 55 to a start shift; no doubt, increased tax revenue will come in. The federal government's new rule would just be binding on states if it gets the job done.

In brief, the Bank of Japan raised its official interest rates on Tuesday and said policymakers would continue holding "reasonable accommodation" with inflation expectations.

The central banker lowered the money rate to a five percent range since October 2013. It's the biggest rise in over 20 years, ahead of Friday's closely watched August reading. Mr Kurodai has said the central bank would hold the finance rate at low levels until it felt inflation improved.

The risk of a second rate hike will be the best incentive to move monetary policy further, Bank of Japan Governor Haruhide Suga said in an interview published on Tuesday. He was responding to the prospect of an increase next month by the Bank of Japan's president, Masayuki Terao.

That could provide a potential trigger for interest rates elsewhere on the globe. The currency fund Global Investors has increased its bet that Japanese stocks would improve heading into the global market in 2017, but there has been no sign of such acceleration in Tokyo's markets.

Should the central banker persist in any of the measures, it could open the way to raising interest rates across the board next year: the 50 basis points that currency rate was set at on Tuesday as a stimulus, and the 100 basis points that the two-percent target is set at. Both sets of notes, as pointed out by the Bank of Japan in their statement on Tuesday, are considered "zero-rate instruments".

Any change would happen in the winter quarter, when some banks, including Japanese banks, will come to believe that a doubling in interest rates – or more aggressive ones – would be the main event. Such a rise is therefore expected in 2016 — the last year of a two-year sovereign-debt-to-GDP ratio bond-buying program.

Indeed, the interest rate shift is a prospect that faces hurdles.

They include the extent to which this is an accommodation with the growth model for businesses in Japan, in which high-income earners, especially those in the middle and working-class, have become increasingly indebted. Such forces, such as increasing costs, are likely to dampen investment inflation.

And that's also the most appealing thing about the recent move. While still in its infancy in the era of the BoJ, the prospect of a rise has been a hot topic in the global exchange-traded fund world, where policymakers are not shy about imposing their own austerity policies.

One source of revenue for investment banking is loans based around fixed rate mortgage-backed securities or fixed rate derivatives. Many investors take out loans to extend mortgages. The interest rates are agreed in advance, but no assurance is given as to financial viability when the contract is announced. There is the risk that a borrower will default on their purchases in the event of market decline.

Mortgage finance contracts issued by the bank may require buyers and sellers to hold collateral of the specific currency used for delivery of the loan, such as US Dollars. The key aspect of investing is: Who pays the dividends? Investors usually hold stock only to cover dividends during tax season. But companies sell off shares and cash. This is called "cash flow" and when you flip stock on an exchange or realize dividends due you "cash out." The amount of cash left to invest is called the "cash on hands." Investors often hold cash as the future earnings depend on "cash flow." What's the concept? If you are holding cash on hand, and you had to pay an income tax payment tomorrow, then the share holder may as a long-run tax expenditure, offsetting the dividends. Likewise, there may also be money left over to sell shares, from dividends received just for buying the dividend stock, to buyback shares from the market, to pay dividends you are not paying. Think about it: You could sell shares, earn cash, and all the profits would go to your account today..... But then the stock would go bust. A selloff. Out. Out. Everyone gets stuck with a loss due to stock price volatility but not any money. But think of "cash on hands"...... Any money left over a year, or even decades ago, will have it's full retirement fund flowing to them, just in case from stock price volatility and interest payments, debt payments and maintenance costs. Why do I put "cash on hands"? You lose patience, at the worst and especially as corporate greed increases or decreases the profit. What if at some point in time there was a deal you couldn't sell your stock for all your clients wouldn't get any cash? What if your dividend payment required you sell your stock? You would not make you people's money. Because that sale money is part of your investment portfolio. Over time, the interest payments roll in. You know you invest in stocks, but having investors pay interest to extract profit from your funds (and more tax savings) makes you wait longer to buy. Think about it.... Even though you give your employer 100% in the event you get laid off in 2003, you will still get paid less in 2004. That's because 90% of your value as an employee or owner is tied up the moment you get laid off. If you're paying your own salaries, you could wait much longer to buy - especially if your hourly job is even in the black as stock prices are volatile. The ULTIMATE economic factor is money. Plus, as each dollar you saved from holding stocks, from investing your money, from dividend payments with

Prior to this week, insurance giant UnitedHealth Group Inc. announced plans to acquire all of the family-owned CareFirst plans.

On Aug. 28, UnitedHealth announced plans to buy CareFirst, the largest non-health insurance company in Virginia, when it trades at a loss of around $3 billion.

In a statement, UnitedHealth said it "has been evaluating opportunities to spin these operations into separate business units to enable more uniform offering across a variety of specialty areas. We will take a further examination and decision regarding this process in the future."

The company said that CareNext "provides direct cost-sharing to all Health Plans under its umbrella, including senior spousal/parental plans, and UnitedHealth network plans offered by its 3 major affiliate insurers," in addition to "general or community coverage available across Healthcare Options Division nationwide."

UnitedHealth will acquire CareFirst's assets, and CareFirst's assets, after CareNext sells its remaining assets. UnitedHealth said the acquisition of CareNext's assets will partially offset the cost of building and operating CareFirst's network services.

Related: Health companies have been buying back shares of healthcare companies. Now, they're buying back Obamacare

In a statement, Susan Wojcicki, UnitedHealth senior vice president and chief investment officer, said the deal helps provide "greater mobility, better benefits, and cost savings" for Virginians.

"Health insurance and Family Blue care now enables a continuum of care that is affordable to millions of people across the nation and even more affordable for those in great need," said Wojcicki. "This transaction will not only help provide relief for caregivers after their loved ones are hospitalized, but also serve the health needs in our patients as they retire, enter old age and maintain support. UnitedHealth has a well-balanced portfolio of high quality and innovative brands, and today's agreement with CareNext will strengthen our existing-value portfolio, and fill both of those gaps."

Shares of UnitedHealth rose more than 5%.

Bloomberg news agency contributed.

Windows Server 2016 suffered a highly cumulative number of system experiences in 2016, impacting on average 64% of our customers. This represents a very serious development for customer experience. In 2016, Windows Server was shipped with 1033 million instances deployed: more (20%) than all of the end users today, and slightly more than all of the consumer end users combined. Our customers lost 800,000 instances and created 3.4 million system pain points. Most of this pain (90%) was centered on Windows Server 2016, and less so on previous Windows products like Windows Server 2000. There was also a significant impact for all end users due to the Windows Server 2016 Anniversary Update, which required system builds missing the 'Fix for 32-bit Server 2008' as they were only present prior to the Windows Server 2015 Anniversary Update. As a result of these well-known issues, on average half the service and error reports related to hardware failed during 2016. On average, the servers ran 3.3-3.4 hours longer than they should have, and needed considerably different attention than usual to resolve. The overall solution of this task required that both PowerShell (in Windows 8.1) and management tools (Windows Server 2016) to adjust frequently and effectively to these frequent outages and maintain system performance had to run at a high level. Oil prices affected employment changes through several pathways. After a crash, employment can be up or down depending on whether the oil price shocks had larger effects on employment. With each shock, capital search contracts can happen. In a crash, firms search for new workers through layoffs during which energy companies buy up labor (or otherwise affect the workers' real wages). With each peak shock, there are low capital prices. The result is growing layoffs, and when oil prices rise new employers can hire. By looking at the time series, the effect is especially pronounced. The oil shock had almost immediate employment effects, and in the final years - at both the most recent peak and median - we could detect a narrowing effect (so small we wouldn't consider it significant). Working to avoid sudden oil price shocks is problematic: the oil price will likely still rise in 2015. Firms are reluctant to hire new workers through layoffs if their oil prices rise. There's plenty to agree on from a business perspective.

Target

Target is quite similar to Amazon both in terms of their ownership of it and their view of its growth. The most obvious parallels, of course, are in its large user base. Targeting targets as a business is pretty straightforward: Find a customer, give him or her a product score and get back to them if they want another, assuming they'll want, for example, that his product be on their shopping list, having it also on their list. Because an Amazon shopper has to order more than five categories at a time, Amazon also offers on-demand transportation for these customers. Our direct competitor, though, sells much more than two categories at a time (ToysRUs).

Target even applies this same formula to its books and other products in its respective region. Moreover, some Target customers don't have the money to buy their own books wholesale, so they pay Target for them via their credit card. This is seen by many as what they describe as "no edge". Both Target and Walmart say similar things.

 in a truly global currency exchange network, for example where payments occur, payments must be confirmed. Commodity traders are affected and their liquidity will suffer. With a currency exchange trading platform, because all transactions are committed to pending orders and clearing, global supply of value matures on a finite relative basis. China and United States agree that cybercrime is a threat, but each country faces its own challenges, and each countries' strengths and weaknesses persist. Here are 10 questions about cybercrime that are going to be crucial to your private and business business dealings.

1. Who Should We Attack?

Not Russia, especially not Russia or China. Anyone knows that people will move to other countries with which they share a culture, economy, and history, especially foreigners, who may share the same ideological outlook and global view. The former Soviet Union was no exception, and many Russians went to, and remain, in China (and other Asian countries that already had their own Communist-style Communist regimes) in search of better opportunities or better life, even if their "guarantors" and "patriotic" supporters might not want them to.

The United States probably faces greater challenges with respect to cybercrime than China. So too are China and Russia, even if they share the USA's stronger reputation in terms of economic development, peacekeeping missions, and national security.

In any case, while I would choose either Russia (where law enforcement and research can be focused on more serious and high-profile forms of fraud and misuse, rather than just theft), which has been extremely aggressive in recent years as the number of cyber attacks has increased, there are too many to take lightly. Not everyone in global cybercrime circles holds these views.

The common factor here suggests that the threats to the industry of businesses would probably better start by focusing on the more subtle forms of crime such as fraud and theft, rather than target the more sophisticated form of cybercrime in which an individual might deliberately damage a company's systems to steal sensitive information or try to convince the company to do something it wouldn't want to do on purpose. This would then provide a much deeper, more effective safety net, which both the US and Russia could implement quickly to reduce the threat of loss.

2. When Will It Be Over?

For now. But, I suspect, the focus of most discussions about cybersecurity and cybercrime will be on their early effects on business, but also less important interactions with current owners of business to be studied in later phases. Why?

This essay introduces the concepts and technique used by the cybersecurity community to solve the security problems.

2. Information Security Basics

The topic of information security has been called "information assurance." Information security describes the information, that humans possess of security, security applications, and problem-solving techniques related to cyber, information systems, and network security. Information security security is a major concern as information travels by bulk, from cyber to security to identity tracking systems and biometric monitoring to law enforcement agencies. What exactly does an information security community mean?

Information security is the collection of information and means of protecting. The need for all information to be accurate and secure is immense. If it is not, the country in which the information originates and flows cannot survive – but we will put up with a lack of information security for more than a day in order " to protect us from the bad guys." Information security techniques are the means to make sure the information flow stays secure from any enemy of information security.

The question of what information security is and how it is defined must be answered for most people. In short, "information security by other than data." In contrast, information security is also (partially) defined by computer systems.

Computers are software. Some form of software is called "computer code." This is the data and information they store and what they process – basically, code is code. The computer code includes the definition, assembly language,, logic, and related instructions.

People tend to call their computers "software" if, for example, they are operating on a file-based environment. This kind of a " software " is useful as it lets someone with less technical skills know how to get things done without moving those skilled

This essay discusses computer security principles and applications. By using well thought-out technical concepts, we develop knowledge about cyber security by making sensible choices that will provide users with the skills, tools, and knowledge they need in order to protect themselves against cyber threats. A high level understanding of computer systems, security exploits, and risks will help protect the wider society from malicious attacks, ransomware, and other forms of cybercrime.

What Is Cybersecurity?

Cybersecurity is the security measure that controls how, when, and where information can be transmitted and stored. It is the protection against cyber fraud, by identifying and cleaning up copies of stolen data. It is the way to protect your data and intellectual property, which is usually stored on services that you use.

Cybercrime relies on a threat.

How can I be sure what was done with my data and intellectual property? Not every kind of crime is by itself so serious that anyone should panic and let the state intervene. An online troll sending a barrage of spam, for example, probably won't hurt anyone (outside crime-fighting organisations), but won't stop thieves from using it as a weapon. Ad hoc "zero-day" hacks (not necessarily malicious enough or more dangerous to pass through an agency's security team) might convince those interested in protecting against cyberthreats to crack the system, but if they are truly successful they might also make personal data accessible to everyone (and therefore the legitimate victim). Security engineers will be so busy with the cyber threat that they are not as active at tackling and fixing it over the long haul, by which time the hacker could have developed a way of extending his activities and spreading his methods to every consumer online. But, even if the hacker didn't get past this first phase, the first stop in his road to making money laundering easier would be using the data-mineing strategy—the sophisticated exploit that could steal your password or access other services more easily—to buy insurance that you're safe. That is, to provide a real barrier between customers and businesses. But as threats become more advanced and sophisticated, this isn't always the best way to go.

How is cybercrime regulated?

We think it's fair to say that it's one of those areas where it's not clear where policy comes from, or where the government is most interested in improving security. What is IRC? (Wikipedia)

IRC was an Internet chat system popularised by the Internet News Network for mailing list service newsgroups originally founded by David Skousen and Jonathon Edwards. It replaced the AOL chat system, which at that time was the norm for most Internet communication. This was because IRC could be set up without advertising, and because it was relatively inexpensive compared with the AOL mail system.

The connection network is the backbone between computers and Internet, storing bits of information about their connections. For example, TCP and UDP packets leave UDP ports if they arrive over TCP network using the RTT to establish a connection. TCP provides a bridge between remote boxes and your computer. It works like a driver that allows remote machines access to your system from afar, and contains instructions from your computer to your remote system on how your computer to communicate between different systems.

This connection network is built to work well with low resources computer on small computers. For example, allow your neighbors and friends to access his computer from afar (without your computer using UDP packet).

Data loss

System switches and routers take files called IP connections, whose locations are scanned and saved regularly during install or update to IP addresses. If these files are scanned for damaged computer or information, there's protection against unwanted IP traffic because IP address changes do not affect your network and server.

A bad IP address can result in accidental internet traffic loss. This happened to me recently, and my home Internet connection stopped working, because unexpected small bits of data—like videos and images—were uploaded. It only took a moment for my equipment manager to scan my computer for IP address issues, and sent me the IP address correcting the errors.

A security problem

All PCs usually come with built-in encryption that protects files and data. A system encrypts all data that can be read without a corresponding keys. Protect yourself from getting any lost material with online data encryption that does not leave keys open anymore.

In brief, Apple has released iOS 8.4, and with it comes new apps for "macOS X High Sierra" along with the other two minor "fixup" jobs for the highly-anticipated new operating system. The new features are particularly evident with iOS 8.2. The problem—quite possibly yet another Apple decision made even with iOS 8.1 and older—is that Apple doesn't make iOS 8.2 for MacOS X even if it fixes it for iOS 8 and therefore effectively cancels all previous work for it.


In simplest terms, iOS 8.3 and its many minor fixes and improvements applied to macOS Sierra are no longer official support for iOS 8. Either these new features aren't needed for High Sierra, or Apple has ignored them completely.


But this new Apple OS update for iOS 8 only fixes a small category of issues that have been hidden with previous updates. Some of its fixes are obvious from the technical side without jumping in too deep to dive inside code specifics (say, you cannot keep tapping the Spotlight button on an app's home screen). But some of it is more subtle (like the new search bar appearing in Safari when more than one application is installed), makes no substantive changes, and still more important than likely has that extra feature (like improved autocompletion of web-mode text).


And this list of hidden items for High Sierra won't include the two major bug fixes for iOS 8, or even the minor "remix" of the current app to install new apps in iOS 7 that Apple implemented for High Sierra. Sure, Apple has had its iOS 8 updates about MacOS X longer than we thought, but not today. Solar cells offer an open-source, no-corporate solution for mass production, and have been used in solar cells in labs across the globe since the 1980s. But now, more than half of those manufactured worldwide rely on Chinese cells, according to a new report. In fact, nearly 40 percent of the solar cells manufactured in North America are found in China.

"Because of its complexity, solar cells typically aren't developed worldwide. It's expensive for a small company like NewBio to develop a new cell," said Joseph Dye, president of MITRE/Myriad Corp.'s mobile business, at the E3 conference in Los Angeles on Wednesday. Microsoft founder Bill Gates, he added, "put solar cells on his back [and] took on the challenge, and in doing so, he sold us a whole new industry."

More than half the cells that power consumer electronics today are made in China, according to Ameren Solar's founder and managing director, Ken Heineman. And two Chinese companies are moving at rush hour to fill growing demand for solar cells, investing in research and development. The big advantages of a solar cell are light and can produce enough energy to power a bulb for up to eight hours. But it comes with a price tag. So a handful of Chinese companies, including NewBio and IBM, are making these cells in large, multinational factories to reduce costs, improve their yield and help them stay in use longer.

NewBio has sold more than 100 such cells so far, and was recently acquired by SunEdison, while IBM is working toward selling some 100 million solar panel systems by 2016. Its partnership with a Taiwanese state-owned firm, NREL, saw its panels sold to over 8 million people around the world.

The central theme behind Microsoft's Surface tablet is the concept that, within a tablet, a user can interact with content in a natural, natural way, without having to go through a UI; an ability to stream two-way stereo audio or use Microsoft Edge browser onscreen to pick up messages as they happen.

For most of us today, the way experiences are delivered and connected through our devices are limited and controlled by companies that can make money from the direct connection between client devices and information and data in the content they show back to the user. They are not the users.

Microsoft's Surface tablet is a break with the past about how we purchase and interact with information and data through digital devices. But it also offers a glimpse—perhaps a glimpse—of what what the future may hold. This is not a new statement, and a previous version of this article explored just how far mobile devices will come.

The picture offered by Microsoft's Surface tablets is not limited to the PC market and the phone world.

Over those few years, Microsoft has built a sizable user base, the kind of consumers who seek new ways to stream content through apps regardless of operating system or device. The general user has come to expect news, video games, music and some of the other high-end entertainment they have come to expect through their computers and their digital devices, who are about equally or even more interested in physical devices in their pockets. At the same time, their trust in the control of physical devices has certainly eroded to a point where users are getting more used to taking their computing prowess with them everywhere. If you're still having trouble embracing the tablet—the first tablet, really, since the device that would become the Surface Book, eventually came out later in 2015—I think Microsoft has something for you.

While what's in the current Surface, which includes new versions of the tablet's core search and discovery capabilities, Office, plus a redesigned keyboard and trackpad, will surprise many anyone buying a new tablet, it's worth noting that the Surface remains far from the tablet it was designed to be. The laptop-like external tablet has long been in the hands of many if not hundreds of millions of consumers, and given Microsoft's background as one of the key companies investing in connected devices in the past decade, it's part and parcel of the company's identity.

This is a fascinating vision with a bunch of potential pitfalls to ride. Microsoft's public response on the Surface tablets has been to play it safe—

The central theme behind Microsoft's HoloLens is user-centric interface (UI) that turns windows into the way humans interact with the environment

The device opens with many windows, including those that house everything from personal information all the way to applications. So the first thing you get off the HoloLens is an app, called Microsoft Hololens, which can snap photographs, play video, create screenshots and make quick notes. It's a set-it-and-forget-it for what you could do with the HoloLens, but that application seems much more focused than a number of others you could run on top of it.

It's almost as though Microsoft and Samsung are working, in fact, on having your privacy at its core. Your HoloLens data is never (no matter how many times I look to the right) associated with you or stored in the device (so even if your log in info comes in with your real name, it belongs nowhere near you, either). But about 60 times a day HoloLens asks that same question: What do you want from it? Some apps come down to you automatically and some you need specific gestures (don't swipe the touchpad, put yourself behind your phone's ear, like on a Walkman), but they're all wrapped with your input: You ask them, you receive them. The key here is whether you're using a computer display or a holographic display, because HoloLens can also offer basic holographic audio systems, allowing the "inside-out" to be moved.

All this could well be the hook, but it's unlikely: Once you sign up to the Microsoft HoloLens as the user (it's basically the system-provided avatar for them), don't install programs on your HoloLens -- they're just there for the benefit of anyone with the headset, perhaps even from an Oculus Rift vendor, in exchange for your agreement not to run on their gaming hardware. Microsoft is also only allowing developers to use HoloLens in a limited way based on screen resolution: from an estimated 1-inch scale to as large as six inches.

The device also has a 'smart browser'--you turn that on and play video for up to 10 seconds using the touchpad, then go back and forth between apps that include images and real-time information (like photos). Prior to this study, scientists thought that bacteria have only two sets of tiny proteins, called single-strand breaks (SSBs) and double-strand breaks (DSBs). When present at identical temperatures or pressures, SSBs break easily into DPBs. DSBs may remain in solution and when stored in an ice crystal, may persist while temperature or volume rise. This feature caused no interest from scientists in ancient human origins, while a search for ancient microbial communities never turned up any evidence.

Now, researchers report that two more different pathways exist for production of SSBs and DPBs. When proteins from human cells are grown in conditions as high as 42oC, their molecules become SSB-like materials. Such activation produces DPBs that dissolve in the solution and produce bacterial structures with multiple components.

This study suggests that there may be more intermediate stages during the microbial life cycle of early humans leading to their first rise to power. For instance, if high nitrogen fertilization rates were successful during the warmest part of prehistory, as appears likely, then bacteria similar to modern bacteria must have evolved for survival under reduced levels of weathering and cold. But some degree of microclimate control is needed to cause such important events to occur.

To summarise Microsoft's new Windows 10 Creators Update, which is expected to roll out sometime in June, is that it promises PC users the best Windows experience yet and, if you use the new hardware—and it will be tested with consumer laptops from Lenovo, Dell and HP—well, there's a future.

It gives more power to the desktop; it grants power to users who want more power; it takes away the need to use the Windows desktop itself for services like email and instant messaging; and it gives users the option to start work and have the machine run without Internet connection. The bottom line? Now they have a new platform to extend their PC experience or improve security or speed. It also raises the future of the enterprise.

Now, before anyone gets too excited about this because there's been no word on when the Creators Update will hit the consumer PC landscape (aside from the fact that it won't impact anything that's already available), let's review some practical implications.

The most important is that, in this case, Windows 8 starts to stand out. When Windows 8 first came out, consumers mainly used productivity suites in PCs—MoltenFledgling, Windows Ink and Metro Designer—along with Word, Excel and PowerPoint. All these applications were launched in Windows XP, which had a focus on productivity and not functionality such as those that had made traditional PC environments so successful. So it was clear in early adopter discussions that the future of PC Windows was focused around comfort and control rather than robustness. Even so, Windows 8 was still seen as something of a shell for a user experience run on the Web: not intended for office-hungry users. Windows Media Centre, for example, hadn't even been released yet when this Windows 8 announcement was made when it became our new standard (and is just now arriving here with the Creators Update)—so Microsoft's goal was done. But as the Internet changed the way we worked, this future for Windows 8 looked much clearer.

Cue the magic of the coming of Windows 10 Update

Look across the Web these days and you'll find the Windows 10 Creators Update sitting there alongside the new Windows 8 Update and the Windows Store Update. Yet, in the last month or so, Microsoft has started to launch lots of updates aimed at the pro-PC segment. The relationship between science fiction and science reality may be subtle, but there's a good deal of overlap. While science fiction, like reality, tends to skew toward fantasy and realism, science fiction movies are more like fantasy and reality than science fiction and reality, as they continue to do today.

A closer look at the science fact discrepancy between science fiction and reality, set in the post-Cold War era, reveals some interesting information about the relationship between science fiction and reality.

A close examination of the science fiction genre characterizes modern ideas as a product of ideas rather than actual technological developments—and thus, for some, fantasy and reality take on a greater relationship with reality than reality ever does with fantasy. This was most clearly evidenced with films with the very specific label "sci-fi"—not, for instance, this year's postmodern masterpiece, Dune published by Frank Herbert in 1978.

Technological advances (mostly in the form of global intelligence for profit purposes) have occurred thanks in part to the U.N. Space Agreement of 1967, but science fiction has the most direct impact on society because it relates to "reality" as it is and the very idea of a dystopian future on Earth is rooted in the concepts of technological progress and the near future in terms of global economic stability.

"The power of their stories is grounded in something real. The power of technology in our world is to create a way of life for humanity where this new technology, if that's real, is linked to where the country is in relation to the world and hence is not perceived as an accident, but as something most people desire," Lyle Goodman, one of the authors of the New York Times bestseller How to Succeed in Business and State by the Sea notes in a recent interview.

The fact that stories about technological progress rely so heavily on ideas and ideas about technology, and that great technological works of fiction like Tolkien and Brontë reflect this idea, may take some drawing out of our pen."

—Edward Klein, author of The Transhuman Future

Sci-fi is generally, though not uniformly, set in reality—although not a whole lot of readers like to admit in traditional or straight-up philosophical or philosophical-historical terms. Writers are often more comfortable telling stories about scientific notions and inventions on what should be called "postmodern" as opposed to being very specific about the objects to be demonstrated.

The issue focused on Microsoft's Windows operating system's security policies, but Microsoft has so many security updates available to its customers that having an updated network drive will also involve updating all of your installed applications.

Now, in an effort to remove conflicts between installations of Windows 10, including VPNs from one Windows 10 computer to another, Microsoft has implemented a system where there is a Windows 10 installation partition available in the Windows network drive, but Windows cannot use it to access data.

Users running "I want to watch a movie on my PC" should just click "Control Panel," select "Network Connections," then check the "Allow connection via a network drive."

In this new configuration, the network drive should be either a fixed drive or a virtual drive, the latter being required when adding a configuration file.

After proceeding, once an application appears in the network drive it must simply be moved there, creating and using a new connection.

Users who decide to take the route of downloading and installing the Netflix application will still have to install that application, however, giving Netflix an open file stream to the Windows network drive.

Now, we can try to understand where this new approach regarding network drives comes from.

Several experts have pointed out that because Microsoft doesn't want to be known as the "one-stop game for all your network-level apps," it seems as if Microsoft is trying to minimize app installations.

Either way, these new instructions take some of the sting out of the newer, stricter Microsoft Windows 10 installation policy.<|endoftext|>A lot of times companies are stuck between getting more-powerful computers and buying more expensive ones. The issue focused on Microsoft's Windows operating system program development tools, it turns out. Microsoft has announced that developers can now write code for the console console functionality for Windows 10. Previously developers were only allowed to create software for Linux PCs.

Jasper Burd, IT director for Microsoft's Windows and Devices group, told Polygon that the shift in regulation was meant to benefit the software giant, although it's largely aimed at more users. The industry "had a belief that this would increase innovation, and really help grow the platform," he said.

Developers applying for Microsoft's program begin looking at PC, Mac and Windows device projects this month; for Macs, developers generally need to look at IE for Linux scenarios and start downloading the latest code through BitBucket, while Windows developers should look towards Microsoft's Windows App Distribution Services to find an "ideal version," he told Polygon. One of the biggest applications is the 360 business, where Microsoft helps license and streamline developer tools, Burd cited as an example.

The move comes amidst Microsoft's ongoing commitment to Windows 10. Last week, the Redmond, Wash.-based company announced that it had sold between 1.1 million and 1.3 million PCs to developers over two years. Microsoft said in its Spring 2016 quarterly earnings report, that it plans to launch "Windows 8.1 Update 2," which adds support for Windows 10 this year to 1.4 million OS launches this year.

The company has been aware for years of improvements by Linux developers who call the operating system "Linux on Windows." Linux by itself is much larger than Windows, which makes Microsoft's change to "closer" to the PC, it says — perhaps it will include "accelerated" or "closer to non-closer" support for the terminal or other programs.

Some of the modifications are to native Windows functionality, such as opening tasks and windows into additional regions of the keyboard or multiple viewing of pictures or videos simultaneously. This month Microsoft debuted the next iteration of its Notepad program, which adds support for Windows 7 and will compete very aggressively with Pro Text Editors, a commercial tool in development. In this essay, I'd like to explain how Microsoft's Exchange Server 2010 includes a feature designed to minimize the amount of data you retain by protecting communications between servers -- one of the most commonly confused areas when it comes to the security model upon which Microsoft is built.

You can store only what you want to. No need to store your desktop version of Google Docs when you just want the default and "clean" version.

When creating a new Exchange server, Mailbox was designed to allow third party applications to write messages from their mailbox to file. Use of the mailbox feature was left up to Exchange 2007. (While many organizations now use the "default" and "clean" file synchronous format for file messages, that file synchronous format was introduced in Exchange 2007 and can't be used for any messages related to services on external servers that require service workers.)

Another feature that is often overlooked is that Exchange messages that are more than three lines long simply become Web attachments, which are sent to your web browser. From within an Exchange mail client, send emails from the web, and make a calendar entry to your assistant. If you upload a Word document to Office 365, it will be as if you wrote this when you uploaded it -- no changes were made, just a document can be open, and Word will start from scratch.

The messages have to be saved within the document itself. That's because it makes sense for Exchange not to be run in isolation from the rest of the system. Office 365 asks you to download your messages and handle them; Microsoft needs them to be saved on a server and available for Exchange to manage.

But even for this kind of work, Exchange messages often include configuration details that are not even part of the standard mail message format. So, while Microsoft has now created powerful capabilities available for managing Exchange workloads across multiple servers -- Exchange 2010 can manage your user settings, Active Directory environment objects, Active Sync configuration files and so on -- some Mailbox features can be abused and cause confusion when using Exchange 2010. These inbound and outbound attachments can easily be modified to make them reveal more information about you and the contacts you have. In this essay, Microsoft researcher Nick Weaver explores Windows Vista's security features. Hardware compatibility and performance limits, and whether to use security only on a network connection or on storage drives, shape our decision making.

Windows Vista is a feature packed operating system and has been designed with protection at its heart. What do I need to know? Because Vista has been shown to offer a reasonable amount of performance in the Web browser test, it seems logical to consider whether I would want to upgrade to version Vista without any prior knowledge of the functionality of the feature set.

Install the Windows Vista Service Pack 1 (SP1) bundle on a Windows Server 2003 installation. Click "Next" and follow the prompts.

Security only protection is one of the software components included on this product. This upgrade will be made available only to Microsoft customers who recently purchased Vista. All additional features may be available at lesser cost to those still running Vista.

In 2001, Microsoft extended the support for vista with new applications and protections that add to the security of Windows Vista despite the fact that some of the older software on Windows XP still runs on Vista. No matter your Internet service, these features are designed to extend the security of Microsoft products, not all of which will run on Vista.

Recommendation [ edit ]

Vista supports Internet connections that support IPsec (IPSec), TLS and 256 ports. A security requirement on the Internet or by a security professional allows for connection of certain Internet-based networks from an IPsec VPN, such as Cisco's Linksys LSI with IPv6 and NetGear's Metra to Vista while still protecting your home network (if it supports VLANs) even though you cannot connect to your home IP. These additional options provide more effective port security in general, while security on Vista is less important, provided your new network is active and security experts like security expert Larry Boyd are around to help you.

Browsers Microsoft offers four standards extensions to support Vista: Firefox 4 , Microsoft Internet Explorer 6 , Microsoft Internet Explorer 7 , and Microsoft Internet Explorer 8. Extensions in Vista allow browsers to accept and render video.
